---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path (long panel: one row per unit-period)
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_event_panel.csv")

  # Variables
  unit_var: "school_id"        # panel unit (fixed effect + clustering)
  time_var: "period"           # calendar period (fixed effect)
  event_time_var: "event_time" # time relative to treatment (…-2,-1,0,1,2…)
  treat_var: "treated"         # treated group flag (1/0, or "Treatment"/"Control")
  outcome_var: "test_score"    # numeric outcome

  # Reference (omitted) event time; its coefficient is pinned to 0.
  ref_period: -1

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Test Score"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Dynamic Impact: Event-Study Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 5.5,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

if (!requireNamespace("fixest", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("The 'fixest' package is required for the event-study plot. ",
       "Run renv::restore() from the project root, then re-render.", call. = FALSE)
}
suppressMessages(library(fixest))   # attach so i() resolves inside the formula

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params.
# The DiD estimation + event-study plot live in viz_event_study()
# (2_R/2.6_viz_functions.R).
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])
```


```{r plot-event-study}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.6_viz_functions.R"), local = TRUE)

# Thin wrapper: map the YAML params into viz_event_study() and print the ggplot
# it returns. Empty-string text params keep the auto-generated defaults.
viz_event_study(
  data           = raw_data,
  unit_var       = params$unit_var,
  time_var       = params$time_var,
  event_time_var = params$event_time_var,
  treat_var      = params$treat_var,
  outcome_var    = params$outcome_var,
  ref_period     = params$ref_period,
  metric_name    = params$metric_name,
  chart_title    = params$chart_title,
  chart_subtitle = params$chart_subtitle,
  x_label        = params$x_label,
  y_label        = params$y_label,
  source_note    = params$source_note
)
```
